context: Tests whether the agent follows the implement-skill workflow for a Vaadin/jOOQ use case: reading the spec and entity model, using Records.mapping() for projected queries with correct column order, using fetchInto(Order.class) only for full-row fetches, and not creating test classes.

checklist.name: Records.mapping() for grid query
checklist.description: The repository method that returns order summary rows uses Records.mapping(...) (with a constructor reference) rather than fetchInto(SomeDto.class) for the projected multi-column SELECT

checklist.name: No fetchInto for projected DTO
checklist.description: The code does NOT call fetchInto(OrderSummaryDto.class) or any other DTO class (only fetchInto(Order.class) for the full-row fetch is acceptable)

checklist.name: Column order matches DTO constructor
checklist.description: The columns selected in the jOOQ projected query appear in the same order as the constructor parameters of the DTO/record used with Records.mapping()

checklist.name: fetchInto(Order.class) for full-row
checklist.description: When fetching a complete order record (e.g. for the side-panel detail), the code uses ctx.selectFrom(ORDERS).fetchInto(Order.class) or equivalent — not Records.mapping()

checklist.name: No test class created
checklist.description: No Java class with a name ending in Test or IT is produced; no file is placed under a test source directory

checklist.name: Data layer separate from view
checklist.description: A Spring @Repository class (e.g. OrderRepository) is created separately from the Vaadin view class

checklist.name: Vaadin view exists
checklist.description: A class annotated with @Route is created for the order history screen (extends VerticalLayout or similar Vaadin layout pattern)

checklist.name: View wired to repository
checklist.description: The Vaadin view class receives the repository via constructor injection (@Autowired or Spring constructor injection) and calls it to populate the grid

checklist.name: Item count in projection
checklist.description: The projected SELECT includes an aggregated item count (e.g. count(ORDER_ITEMS.ID) or DSL.count()) joining or correlating with ORDER_ITEMS
